description: Long blog post with recipe buried in prose — tests noise extraction
sourceText:
Source title:
My Coffee Journey: How I Finally Nailed Pour-Over

Page text:
My Coffee Journey: How I Finally Nailed Pour-Over
by Sarah's Coffee Corner

I've been a coffee lover for as long as I can remember. It started with instant coffee in college, moved on to a basic drip machine, and eventually I discovered the world of specialty coffee. Let me tell you, it was a game changer.

I remember the first time I walked into a third-wave coffee shop. The barista was making a pour-over with a Kalita Wave and I was mesmerized. The precision, the care, the aroma — it was beautiful. I knew I had to learn.

I bought my first Kalita Wave about six months ago. The first few brews were terrible. Too sour, too bitter, too watery. I watched probably 50 YouTube videos trying to get it right. I read blogs, joined Reddit communities, and asked every barista I met for advice.

Finally, after weeks of experimentation, I found my recipe. Here it is:

Sarah's Kalita Wave Recipe:
- 20g coffee, medium grind (I use a Baratza Encore at setting 16)
- 320g water at 92°C
- Kalita Wave 185 with Wave filter

Steps:
1. Pour 50g to bloom, wait 30 seconds
2. At 0:30, slowly pour to 180g (about 45 seconds of pouring)
3. At 1:30, pour to 320g (about 30 seconds)
4. Wait for full drawdown

Total time should be 3:00 to 3:30.

This recipe has been my go-to for months now. I've tried tweaking it dozens of times but keep coming back to these exact parameters. The key insight for me was temperature — 92°C is the sweet spot for the light roasts I prefer.

If you're just starting out with pour-over, my advice is: be patient, take notes, and don't be afraid to experiment. Happy brewing!
